title: Arthrophyllum elmeri
intro: Arthrophyllum elmeri, also known as Elmer's Arthrophyllum, is a species of flowering plant in the family Araliaceae, native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Arthrophyllum elmeri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Arthrophyllum elmeri is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, often in combination with other herbs, to treat respiratory and fever-related conditions.
taxonomy: Arthrophyllum elmeri is classified as a species of plant in the family Araliaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Arthrophyllum, which consists of various species of ferns. The classification further includes the kingdom Plantae, with the division of Pteridophyta. As a member of the Pteridophyta division, Arthrophyllum elmeri is a type of fern. It is a rare and endemic species found in specific regions.

The leaves of Arthrophyllum elmeri are used in traditional medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia, to treat various health conditions.
